publications.title: Structural insights into clonal restriction and diversity in T cell recognition of two immunodominant SARS-CoV-2 nucleocapsid epitopes.
publications.authors: Yuan P, Chen G, Li Y, Liu X, Saravanakumar S, Zhao J, Ji Q, Wang H, Lin YW, Elbahnasawy M, Weng NP, Pierce BG, Mariuzza RA, Wu D.
publications.executive_summary: This study elucidates the structural basis for divergent T cell recognition of two immunodominant SARS-CoV-2 nucleocapsid epitopes—LLL presented on HLA-A2 and SPR presented on HLA-B7—by determining crystal or cryo-EM structures of TCR-peptide-MHC complexes for each epitope. The LLL epitope elicits a clonally restricted response driven by highly constrained Vα gene usage, while SPR generates a diverse T cell response accommodated by multiple structural solutions in the TCR binding interface. These findings are significant for vaccine design targeting the conserved nucleocapsid protein, which has greater cross-variant potential than the spike protein, and they provide structural frameworks for predicting T cell responses across SARS-CoV-2 variants and related coronaviruses. The structures also reveal strengths and limitations of AlphaFold models for TCR-pMHC prediction, advancing computational approaches in T cell immunology.
